contraventionDescription: Parked in a pay and display car park, without clearly displaying a valid pay and display ticket – Contravention Code 83

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
1. We hear of penalty charge notices being issued for this offence even when the pay and display tickets are clearly visible! It may be that you had displayed it in a side window, attached it to the front windscreen or simply placed it on the dash board. Sometimes the adhesive fails and it is possible for a ticket, especially in damp weather, to fall off of the windscreen and land upside down. Sometimes the window fogs up on the inside and the ticket is not visible. If you have a pay and display ticket you should send it in to the local authority confirming that it either was displayed clearly or that due to the failure of the adhesive it had fallen off of the windscreen, etc.
Below we show diagrams of the signs which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
2. We have heard of instances of motorists who did not display their ticket, borrowing tickets from other motorists and sending it in with a representation as proof that they had in fact displayed the ticket, although of course we cannot condone such actions.
3. Are the instructions clearly visible and understandable on the pay and display machine? If the controlled hours are not on the signs were you able to see the details on the machine. Was it dark and a streetlight not working? Did you think that you didn’t need to pay because for example it was late at night and you couldn’t see the hours clearly on the machine or the signs? Were the terms and conditions clearly visible? If they are not then you should appeal. Also check that the charge fee shown and the penalty on the signs and machines is the same as sometimes machines/signs are not updated with the new charges or indeed the new fines for non compliance .If there is any discrepancy then you should appeal. The local authority has an obligation to make sure that the hours of enforcement and the signs advising that you are in a pay and display car park and the terms and conditions are clearly visible.
4. If you believe the machine to be defective, ask the local authority for a copy of the maintenance record for that machine as any previously reported records of malfunction should be logged. It may have been that you attempted to pay but the machine would not accept your money.
5. Please remember that you may cite compassionate grounds when appealing against a penalty charge notice.
6. Signs in the car park may well vary from car park to car park however the charges, the hours of operation, the terms and conditions and level of the penalty for non compliance must be clearly displayed.
 
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
Back to Parking
